title: Lasting Marriage | #shorts
full_text: My mother-in-law lives in India but comes to America for three months at a time, right? It's like she has her own season. Winter is coming. And the mother-in-law relationship in our culture is very is very interesting here in America. When people get engaged or married, they exchange the rings between the two people, right? Who making the commitment? Not so about this necklace. This necklace, even though it's a symbol of marriage, is usually a gift from the mother-in-law to the daughter-in-law. So, you can imagine how much love goes into it. And one time, my friend Sema was getting married and she got a beautiful Mongal Sutra with diamonds and even a ruby, right? And we were all so jealous. And then we found out that the day after the wedding, the mother-in-law took it back. [laughter] She said, "Are you crazy? This necklace wasn't for you. It was just for the photos." So I said, "Seema, good thing I wasn't there. Otherwise, I would have asked her, "Do I keep the husband or is he also just for the photos?
